title: General Patient Treatment Consent
description: A standard patient intake consent form permitting healthcare professionals to administer diagnostic tests, physical exams, and basic clinical treatments.
whatsCovered: Consent for physical examinations and diagnostic tests
Financial responsibility for co-pays and insurance billing
Acknowledgement of privacy practices and HIPAA rules
whenToUse: Use this consent form as a standard document during patient onboarding or registration at a new medical or wellness practice.

clauses.heading: PATIENT CONSENT AND OBLIGATION
clauses.body: This Patient Consent is signed by {patient_name}, DOB: {patient_dob} ("Patient"), in favor of {clinic_name} ("Clinic"), as of {effective_date}.

clauses.heading: 1. Consent to Routine Care
clauses.body: Patient hereby authorizes Clinic's medical staff to perform physical examinations, diagnostic tests, lab draws, and routine clinical treatments as deemed necessary by Patient's providers.

clauses.heading: 2. Financial Responsibility
clauses.body: Patient understands that they are ultimately responsible for all clinical charges, co-pays, and deductibles not covered by their health insurance provider.

clauses.heading: 3. Privacy Practices Acknowledgement
clauses.body: Patient acknowledges receiving Clinic's Notice of Privacy Practices, which describes how their medical information may be used and disclosed under HIPAA regulations.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This consent document shall be interpreted and governed under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Can I refuse a specific test?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a general consent. You always retain the right to refuse any specific test, draw, or treatment proposed by your physician.

faq.question: Who bills my insurance?
faq.answer: The clinic will bill your insurance provider as a convenience, but any unpaid balances remain your financial responsibility.
